label: SES-136 / CNV-038 close-out — PRJ-015 kickoff + first usability improvement (references rendered as an enriched sortable/filterable grid). Creates and resolves PI-113. Records three decisions (amend the unapplied 0030 migration in place; server-side reference enrichment; references-as-grid layout + columns) and two commits already on main (8421345d migration fix + PRJ-015 creation; 544a40f references grid). Belongs to Project PRJ-015.

session.session_title: PRJ-015 kickoff and first usability improvement — references rendered as an enriched sortable/filterable grid
session.session_executive_summary: A Claude Code session that established Project PRJ-015 (an umbrella for incremental V2 desktop-UI usability work) and delivered its first improvement. Creating PRJ-015 surfaced a latent bug in the committed-but-never-applied migration 0030 (it mutated change_log workstream→project rows while the stale CHECK was still active), which was fixed in place and applied, unblocking governance creates. The improvement itself replaces the references widget's identifier+type text links — which made records like PI-048 unrecognizable — with a single sortable/filterable grid carrying Direction, Relationship, Identifier, Type, Title, Status, Created, and Updated, fed by a new server-side per-edge other_summary enrichment. 876 tests pass; both commits are on main.
session.session_description: Seed prompt: "I would like to define a new project for CRMBuilder V2." The session opened by orienting on the V2 governance state and the post-PI-112 Project model, then created Project PRJ-015 "UI usability improvements" via POST /projects per governance-recording-rules §2 (Projects are authored directly, not via close-out). The first POST 500'd: migration 0030 (committed in the PI-112 Phase-3 line, never successfully applied, so the engagement DB was stuck at 0029) ran its workstream→project change_log UPDATEs while the old ck_changelog_entity_type CHECK still forbade 'project', and on downgrade recreated constraints before reversing data. Fixed by reordering both directions to drop CHECK → mutate unconstrained data → recreate CHECK; applied to the CRMBUILDER engagement DB (now at head 0030), reversibility proven on a throwaway copy, and PRJ-015 then created cleanly. The session also documented that the live API reads a per-engagement DB at crmbuilder-v2/data/engagements/CRMBUILDER.db via CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH (not the stale data/v2.db default). The improvement was scoped with Doug across three design choices (enrichment approach, layout, columns) and built in two slices: (1) access/entity_summary.py — a per-entity-type registry mapping all 27 ENTITY_TYPES members to title/status/created/updated columns, with an import-time coverage assert plus an introspection test, consumed by references.list_touching to attach an other_summary block to every edge; (2) a rewrite of the shared ReferencesSection widget from a QLabel link list to a QTableView over a QSortFilterProxyModel — sortable headers, an all-column filter box, sized-to-content for the detail pane's scroll area, with navigation moved to row double-click and the right-click menu, Add-reference button, exclude_relationships filter, read-only mode, and constructor signature all preserved. Affected widget/navigation/panel tests were updated to drive the grid; the full affected surface is 876 passing tests. Two commits landed on main: 8421345d (0030 fix + PRJ-015 creation) and 544a40f (references grid). The API + UI were restarted so the enriched payload is served live and verified. PI-113 captures the references-grid improvement and is resolved by this session.

conversation.conversation_title: Define PRJ-015 and build its first improvement — references as an enriched grid
conversation.conversation_purpose: Stand up Project PRJ-015 as an umbrella for V2 desktop-UI usability work, then deliver its first item: replace the references widget's text-link rendering with a sortable/filterable grid that shows each related record's key fields.
conversation.conversation_description: Created PRJ-015 via direct POST; diagnosed and fixed the 0030 change_log migration's statement ordering (drop CHECK → mutate data → recreate CHECK, both directions), applied it to the engagement DB, and proved reversibility. Resolved three design decisions with Doug (server-side enrichment; unified sortable/filterable grid; column set Direction/Relationship/Identifier/Type/Title/Status/Created/Updated). Built slice 1 (access/entity_summary.py + list_touching other_summary enrichment, registry guarded by coverage + introspection tests) and slice 2 (ReferencesSection rewritten to a QTableView + QSortFilterProxyModel preserving navigation, context menu, add/delete, exclude, and read-only behavior). Updated the widget/navigation/processes/crm_candidates/entities tests to drive the grid; 876 pass. Restarted the API + UI and verified the enriched payload live. PI-113 created and resolved.

planning_items.title: References rendered as an enriched sortable/filterable grid (first PRJ-015 usability improvement)
planning_items.description: The shared ReferencesSection widget rendered each relation as an identifier+type text link, so a reader could not tell what a referenced record (e.g. PI-048) actually was, nor see its status or dates, without navigating away. Replace that rendering, across all ~20 panels that embed the widget, with a single sortable/filterable grid showing Direction, Relationship, Identifier, Type, Title, Status, Created, and Updated. Requires server-side enrichment: references.list_touching attaches an other_summary block (the far side's title/status/created/updated) resolved by a per-entity-type registry. The widget becomes a QTableView over a QSortFilterProxyModel (sortable headers + all-column filter box), preserving double-click navigation, the right-click Delete/Go-to menu, the Add-reference button, the exclude_relationships filter, and read-only mode.
planning_items.executive_summary: First usability improvement under PRJ-015. Reference relations were unrecognizable identifier+type links; this turns them into a sortable, filterable grid that surfaces each related record's title, status, and create/update dates inline. Delivered as a server-side enrichment of the references-touching payload plus a rewrite of the shared references widget to a table, so the change lands uniformly across every entity panel that shows references.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-112 — fix 0030 change_log migration statement ordering; create PRJ-015
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-112 — fix 0030 change_log migration statement ordering; create PRJ-015

Migration 0030 ran the workstream→project change_log UPDATEs while the old
CHECK was still active (and recreated constraints before reversing data on
downgrade), so `alembic upgrade head` failed and any new Project create 500'd.
Reorder to drop CHECK → mutate data → recreate CHECK in both directions.
Applied to the CRMBUILDER engagement DB; regenerated db-export snapshots
(incl. PRJ-015 "UI usability improvements").

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PRJ-015 — references as enriched sortable/filterable grid
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PRJ-015 — references as enriched sortable/filterable grid

First usability improvement under PRJ-015. References were rendered as
identifier+type text links, so you could not tell what (e.g.) PI-048 was,
nor see its status or dates, without navigating away.

Server (slice 1): new access/entity_summary.py maps every ENTITY_TYPES
member to its title/status/created/updated columns; references.list_touching
attaches an `other_summary` block (far-side record's key fields) to each
edge. Coverage is guarded by an import-time assert + introspection test, so
adding a vocab type without a mapping fails the suite.

UI (slice 2): ReferencesSection rewritten from a QLabel list to a single
QTableView (columns: Direction, Relationship, Identifier, Type, Title,
Status, Created, Updated) over a QSortFilterProxyModel — sortable headers,
a filter box matching across all columns, sized-to-content for the detail
pane's scroll area. Navigation moves to row double-click; right-click menu
(Delete + Go to), Add-reference button, exclude_relationships, read-only
mode, and the constructor signature are all preserved.

Tests: new test_entity_summary.py; test_references_section.py rewritten for
the grid (+ filter, sort, missing-summary, enrichment cases); navigation/
processes/crm_candidates/entities panel tests updated. 876 pass.

decisions.title: Fix the unapplied 0030 change_log migration in place rather than authoring a corrective 0031
decisions.context: Creating Project PRJ-015 (POST /projects) 500'd on `CHECK constraint failed: ck_changelog_entity_type`. Migration 0030 (committed in the PI-112 Phase-3 line) was meant to rename change_log's workstream→project entity_type but had broken statement ordering: in upgrade it ran the UPDATE to 'project' while the old CHECK (which forbade 'project') was still active; in downgrade it recreated the old CHECK before reversing the data. Either direction fails `alembic upgrade head`, which is why the engagement DB was stuck at 0029 and every governance create that emits a change_log row for a 'project' failed. The question: amend the existing 0030 file in place, or leave 0030 as-is and ship a new corrective migration 0031.
decisions.decision: Amend 0030 in place — split the drop and recreate of ck_changelog_entity_type into separate batch operations around the data UPDATEs, in the order drop CHECK → mutate unconstrained data → recreate CHECK, symmetrically in upgrade and downgrade. Apply the corrected 0030 to the CRMBUILDER engagement DB and prove reversibility on a throwaway copy.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Author a corrective 0031 that leaves the broken 0030 in history and fixes the constraint afterward. Rejected because 0030 had never been successfully applied to any database (every DB was still at 0029 precisely because 0030 failed), so there is no deployed state that depends on the broken version; amending in place yields a clean, correct migration chain with no dead corrective step, and a fresh clone's `alembic upgrade head` simply works. A corrective 0031 would have left a permanently-broken 0030 that no DB could pass through, making the chain un-runnable from scratch.
decisions.consequences: 0030 now drops the CHECK, runs the workstream→project / WS-→PRJ- rewrites on the 17 historical change_log rows unconstrained, then recreates the CHECK to match the ORM model (ENTITY_TYPES | {'reference'}); downgrade is the exact inverse. The CRMBUILDER engagement DB is at head 0030 and serves governance creates for Projects. Any other DB still at 0029 picks up the corrected migration on next upgrade. Recorded alongside the discovery that the live API uses the per-engagement DB at crmbuilder-v2/data/engagements/CRMBUILDER.db.
decisions.executive_summary: How to fix the broken 0030 change_log migration that blocked all new Project creation. Amend it in place (reorder to drop the CHECK, mutate data, then recreate the CHECK) rather than shipping a separate corrective 0031. Safe because the buggy 0030 had never applied anywhere — no deployed state depends on it — so editing it keeps the migration chain runnable from scratch instead of leaving a permanently-failing step in history.

decisions.title: Enrich references server-side with an other_summary block rather than fetching related records in the UI
decisions.context: To render a references grid showing each related record's title, status, and dates, the UI needs those fields — but /references/touching returns edge data only (source/target type+id+relationship). The fields live on ~25 heterogeneous entity tables with different column names. The enrichment could be done server-side (extend list_touching to join the far side's key fields onto each edge) or client-side (the UI fetches each related record after getting the edges, dispatching per type).
decisions.decision: Server-side. references.list_touching attaches an other_summary block ({identifier, entity_type, title, status, created_at, updated_at}) for the far side of every edge, resolved by a new access/entity_summary.py registry mapping each ENTITY_TYPES member to its title/status/created/updated columns. One round-trip; reusable by the API and MCP, not just the desktop UI.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Client-side batch fetch — after listing edges, the UI fetches the related records (batched by type via the existing get_<type> client methods) and joins them in. Rejected because it adds round-trips per detail render, pushes a ~25-type dispatch into the UI layer, and would not benefit any non-UI consumer (API/MCP) of the touching endpoint; the per-type column knowledge belongs in the access layer next to the models, where an introspection test can guard it.
decisions.consequences: New module access/entity_summary.py with a _SPECS registry (23 enriched types) plus a _NO_SUMMARY set (charter, status, catalog_entity, catalog_attribute) that resolve to None; coverage is asserted at import and by test_entity_summary.py, and an introspection test fails if any mapped column is renamed. Enrichment is one lookup per edge (N+1 across a record's references) — bounded for a detail pane and left simple; batching by type is a possible later optimization. Process and deposit_event surface their classification/outcome in the status slot; topic and commit have no status.
decisions.executive_summary: Where to resolve the related record's title/status/dates for the references grid. Server-side — list_touching attaches an other_summary block per edge via a per-entity-type registry — rather than having the UI fetch each related record itself. One round-trip, reusable by API and MCP, and the per-type column mapping lives in the access layer beside the models with a test that fails if a column is renamed.

decisions.title: Render references as one unified sortable/filterable grid with eight columns
decisions.context: The shared ReferencesSection widget rendered relations as identifier+type rich-text links grouped into per-(direction, relationship) sub-sections. The usability goal is to recognize a referenced record and see its status/dates at a glance, and to sort and filter. Two layouts were on the table: a single unified grid with Direction and Relationship as columns, or keeping the existing kind grouping but rendering each bucket as its own small table. A column set also had to be chosen.
decisions.decision: One unified grid: a single QTableView over a QSortFilterProxyModel with columns Direction, Relationship, Identifier, Type, Title, Status, Created, Updated. Sortable headers; a single filter box matching across all columns; sized-to-content so the detail pane's scroll area handles overflow. Navigation moves to row double-click; the right-click Delete/Go-to menu, the Add-reference button, the exclude_relationships filter, read-only mode, and the constructor signature are preserved.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Grouped mini-tables — keep the current 'Decided in / Supersedes / …' kind grouping with each bucket as its own small sortable table. Rejected because sort and filter would be per-bucket, defeating the goal of scanning or finding a specific relation across all of a record's references at once; the unified grid makes Direction and Relationship first-class sortable/filterable columns. A minimal three-column set (ID/Title/Status) was also considered and rejected in favor of including Direction, Relationship, Created, and Updated for context (Doug elected to add Updated).
decisions.consequences: ReferencesSection rewritten from a QLabel list to a table-backed widget; the change lands in one shared widget so all ~20 embedding panels update uniformly. Tests that asserted the old QLabel rendering (widget unit tests, cross-panel navigation, processes/crm_candidates/entities panels) were updated to drive the grid. Date columns sort chronologically via an ISO sort key in the UserRole; missing summaries render as a dash. Future column or behavior tweaks are localized to this one widget.
decisions.executive_summary: How references should look and behave in the detail pane. One unified sortable/filterable grid (Direction, Relationship, Identifier, Type, Title, Status, Created, Updated) rather than keeping the per-relationship grouping as separate mini-tables. Lets the operator sort and filter across all of a record's references at once, and because it is one shared widget the change applies uniformly to every entity panel.
